description_long: This gatehouse is a small, rectangular stone structure with a red-tiled gable roof, set on a slightly sloped terrain. The building features thick, rough-hewn stone walls and a prominent walled courtyard area attached to one side, enclosed by a lower stone wall. The main entryway is a simple rectangular opening in the front wall, lacking a visible door, and there are minimal window openings, emphasizing a fortified appearance. The roof is constructed from terracotta tiles, giving it a traditional, rustic look. The ground around the gatehouse is uneven, with patches of bare earth and stone visible. The overall design suggests a utilitarian, defensive function typical of medieval or rural gatehouses.
description: A small, rectangular stone gatehouse with a red-tiled gable roof and an attached walled courtyard, featuring a simple rectangular entryway and minimal windows.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the main entryway, the attached walled courtyard, and the sloped terrain.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the full extent of the courtyard enclosure and the back of the building.
description_view_2: Direct rear view displaying the long stone wall of the building and the absence of windows or doors on this side.
description_view_3: Rear-right view showing the side entry and the connection between the building and the courtyard wall.
attribute_entryway_design: Rectangular stone doorway with no visible door.
attribute_window_placement: Minimal small openings, mostly absent or limited to one side.
